title: hieracium cordatum subsp. acanthophyllum
intro: Hieracium cordatum subsp. acanthophyllum is a lesser-known species of the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as Hawkweed, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium cordatum subsp. acanthophyllum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is said to have therapeutic effects when consumed.
